text: The standard Alfredson eccentric heel-drop protocol prescribes 180 repetitions per day (3 sets of 15 reps, twice daily, for both straight-knee and bent-knee variations) for Achilles tendinopathy rehabilitation.
population: nonathletic individuals with midportion Achilles tendinopathy

text: A do-as-tolerated eccentric heel-drop protocol produced equivalent VISA-A and VAS pain outcomes to the standard 180 reps/day Alfredson protocol at 6 weeks, with no statistically significant between-group differences.
population: nonathletic individuals with midportion Achilles tendinopathy

text: The standard 180 reps/day Alfredson protocol caused a within-group deterioration in VISA-A scores at week 3 before improving by week 6, suggesting initial overload that a lower-volume approach avoids.
population: nonathletic individuals with midportion Achilles tendinopathy

text: The do-as-tolerated eccentric heel-drop group showed statistically significant improvement in both VISA-A scores (P<.001) and VAS pain (P=.001) at 6 weeks, while the standard group only reached significance on VISA-A (P=.03).
population: nonathletic individuals with midportion Achilles tendinopathy

text: No adverse effects were reported in either the standard or do-as-tolerated eccentric heel-drop protocol groups over the 6-week intervention period.
population: nonathletic individuals with midportion Achilles tendinopathy

text: Lower repetition volumes of eccentric heel drops, self-regulated by patient tolerance, can be used as an alternative to the full Alfredson protocol without compromising clinical outcomes for midportion Achilles tendinopathy.
population: nonathletic individuals with midportion Achilles tendinopathy
